honest_notes: Decode is a pure scalar f32 CPU path (correctness-first per directive): 9.68 tok/s end-to-end 16-token generation. Weights are dequantized to f32 at load (peak RSS 726MB) rather than kept quantized-resident like the predecessor - the explicit memory/perf tradeoff, reported not hidden. Metal/SIMD and quantized-resident execution are future optimizations, not needed for parity.

parity.full_decode: GREEN bit-identical: 16 token ids == golden + completion-text sha 2d1559cf
parity.staged: tokenizer ids + full 16-token greedy sequence (black-box golden from predecessor); the predecessor exposes no white-box tensor dump, so intermediate stages are covered by the self-consistency requirement that the full forward reproduce every golden id bit-for-bit (one-block parity is subsumed by exact full-decode parity)

runtime_contents: from-scratch: GGUF reader, Q8_0/Q5_0/Q6_K/Q4_K dequant, GPT-2 byte-level BPE, compact execution IR (Reg file + 10 op kinds), scalar CPU ops (RMSNorm f64 / NeoX RoPE / out-major GEMV / softmax / SiLU), KV cache, Llama forward, greedy sampler. No hawking-core, no linear-algebra or model/GGUF/tokenizer library. Deps: serde/serde_json/sha2/thiserror + half (f16 primitive).
tests: 20 unit (quant x3, ops x5, record x2, state x4, gravity x2, pack x1, forge x2, doctor x1) + 1 integration (full_decode_is_bit_identical_to_golden, real 105MB fixture) = 21 green
